verdict_reasoning: IZUMO4 is a member of the IZUMO family of proteins. Unlike IZUMO1 (which has a clear single-pass TM domain and is essential for sperm-egg fusion at the cell surface), IZUMO4 lacks a transmembrane domain and is predicted to be a secreted/soluble protein. It has no documented GPI anchor, TM domain, or stable TM-partner tethering. It is predominantly detected intracellularly or as a secreted factor. Checking contextual buckets: no documented cancer/cell-death outer-leaflet pool; expression is enriched in testis but without established surface display (and even if testis-restricted, it lacks its own membrane anchor); not a lysosomal TM protein; no documented PM minority pool or cycling; no wash-resistant TM-partner tethering reported. The protein is best classified as secreted-only with no stable surface anchoring mechanism.
predicted_key_uncertainty: IZUMO4 is poorly characterized; its precise secretion vs. intracellular localization and any potential TM-partner interaction in germline cells are not well established in the literature.
